reviews.review_description:
First, thank you developers team for this beautiful game.  I enjoy the art style of the environment and characters.  Good jobs.  
Can I as player able to just walk around to view the area by teleport or free locomotion , or walk everywhere.   Just walk slowly to enjoy the environment, to view the art work.  
While I walk around the environment with my bow and arrow,  Allow me to shoot any objects or target within the environment,  objects like balloons,  birds, apples.
This is just a suggestion to have more fun into the game if possible.   
The bow and arrow are fun to play.  
I do recommend this good game to everyone to enjoy.  Thank you.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind worked Bryanlo. We'll consider adding a Zen Mode with the purpose you mentioned as well as mini-games for target practicing.

reviews.review_title: Super fun to play, room for improvement!
reviews.review_description: Ffrom the first moment I started playing Arrows I was hooked! The game is just soooo good! Excellent graphics, fun concept, unique and very well executed... with some minor quirks like arrows often going straight through the enemies heads - and sometimes body - without registering a hit, please fix?! Other than that it's an amazing experience, I will be recommending this game to everyone I know :) Thank you so much and can't wait for the next update!

One of my Favorite games!!! I have played many other games that may have a bit more complexity but I always come back to Arrows. It by far has exceeded any expectation I had, Wish more game were like this!

Awesome alternative to "The Lab" longbow game! Multiple levels are detailed and cute!
Very easy on the stomach, didn't have any motion sickness issues

reviews.review_title: should not be a paid app yet
reviews.review_description:
Love the art style and has real potential but still too many bugs and missing functionality to be a paid app. 
- headshots sometimes show as arrow in head but do not register as hit
- other clearly valid body shots do not register; goes straight through enemy without registering hit
- no way to exit level without quiting game
- need a tutorial to define game mechanics
- setting arrow aflame makes play space not able to be seen so you have to guess instead of aim
- time of day/difficulty may be selected at home screen but level still launches with dawn/easy as time of day

developer_response.body:
Hey GreenTeaBaggins.
Thank you for your honest review. Let me try to help you out. In every level you should be able to either quit back to the main lobby or retry it from the beginning from the pause menu. You can enter the pause menu from your options button on the left controller. 
We're currently working on an update to provide a tutorial for some mechanics as well as to make it clear for players what actions are possible.
Thank you again for raising concerns regarding the selection of difficulty. If you click again on the level after selecting the time of day you should be able to launch it correctly - you should see the level name is updated on the book. We'll add that improvement to the backlog as well.

reviews.review_description:
I am an old man (52) and new to VR. Having been give a Quest 2 as a gift after being diagnosed with illness and relegated to staying at home. I love the VR experience and must say that Arrows is a blast play. The straight forward controls and graphics make this a fun game to have. I do hope that you continue to add onto this experience and keep this game going. Maybe adding some flying creatures into the mix (dragons or something cool. Possibly some mini-games to earn points and get practice, etc.)

The only issue that I had with the game is playing left handed. I grabbed the bow with my left hand in the main menu. But, when the game play started, it automatically switched back to my right and wouldn't correct.

developer_response.body:
First of all, we'd like to wish you a speedy recovery! We're so grateful Arrows can be a positive experience during those days.

Both your ideas of the dragons/flying enemies and mini-games are currently being discussed, thanks for bringing those up.

Let me try to help you with your issue. To start any level you need to grab the bow. You can either grab with the left or right hand depending on your preference. 
It's not obvious in this version but you can drop the bow to switch hands by pressing the Y button (if grabbed with left controller) or B button (if grabbed with the right controller).

We'll look closely if we can reproduce your issue and please reach out to us at aurastudiosvr@gmail.com if you need further assistance. 

Thanks for the support!

reviews.review_description: Love it! Really fun, simple archery game with clean mechanics—great for a new VR player

reviews.review_title: Great fun and a light bit of work!
reviews.review_description: Excellent app - love the concept! Was looking for something like this. Great fun and super gameplay.

This is a fun arrow game, very similar to LONGBOW from the Steam Lab, but better because you can run it directly on the Quest 2 without the cable or a high-end computer.  We are still working our way through the levels and villages, but so far the experience is great.  No glitches of consequence, and the game play is varied enough to keep it fun.  You can move around across different perches and places, shooting your arrows from more or less convenient spots.   And there is a cannon later that helps defeat the giant.  Well done.  And our family really appreciates the low price point, and don't-need-a-cable features.  Much appreciated.
